description: A workstation sent several gigabytes of outbound TCP traffic to an unfamiliar internal host over a long duration with unusually high packet volume.
resolution: Isolate the source host, preserve packet captures, verify the destination owner, rotate exposed credentials, and block the destination until business justification is confirmed.

description: A client generated thousands of packets in under one second while probing multiple TCP services, indicating reconnaissance or automated scanning.
resolution: Rate-limit the source, review endpoint process activity, block scanning tools, and check firewall logs for follow-on connection attempts.

description: Traffic appeared on rare TCP port 31337 with elevated bytes and packets, matching historical backdoor command-and-control behavior.
resolution: Quarantine both endpoints, inspect for persistence mechanisms, block the rare port at network boundaries, and run malware triage on the source.

description: A host initiated high-volume outbound TCP traffic to port 4444, a port commonly used by reverse shell tooling.
resolution: Disconnect the host from the network, collect volatile evidence, terminate suspicious processes, and review recent authentication and PowerShell logs.

description: A source sent repeated UDP traffic with high packet counts to DNS service ports and abnormal byte volume for normal name resolution.
resolution: Inspect DNS query payloads, block suspicious domains, force DNS through approved resolvers, and investigate the source for data staging activity.

description: An internal server transmitted unusually large byte volumes over database ports after a short burst of high packet activity.
resolution: Pause database access for the account, review query logs, verify backup jobs, and rotate credentials if the transfer was not scheduled.

description: A host generated many short TCP sessions with low duration and moderate packet counts toward authentication services.
resolution: Apply temporary source blocking, inspect authentication failures, enforce MFA, and reset accounts with repeated failed logins.

description: ICMP traffic showed unusually high packet counts over a short duration, consistent with a ping flood or availability attack.
resolution: Apply ICMP rate limits, verify service availability, identify upstream sources, and tune monitoring thresholds after containment.

description: A workstation sent abnormal traffic to SSH or remote administration ports outside the normal maintenance window.
resolution: Validate the administrator identity, review privileged session logs, disable unauthorized remote access, and rotate privileged credentials.

description: A device repeatedly contacted an unusual port with consistent packet patterns and byte volume, suggesting automated beacon traffic.
resolution: Block the destination, collect endpoint telemetry, compare timing patterns with threat intelligence, and reimage the host if compromise is confirmed.

description: A web server produced a sudden burst of outbound TCP traffic after short inbound activity on HTTP or HTTPS service ports.
resolution: Review web server access logs, search for recently modified scripts, remove malicious files, patch the vulnerable application, and rotate application secrets.

description: An internal host communicated with several private destinations using high packet counts on administrative or database ports.
resolution: Segment the source host, inspect endpoint detections, review account usage across hosts, and block east-west access that is not explicitly required.
